channel.title: Ruijun Lin on Ruijun Lin's Space
channel.description: Recent content in Ruijun Lin on Ruijun Lin's Space

item.title: About Me
item.description:
I am a PhD student in the School of Mathematics and Physics at the University of Wollongong (UoW), Australia.

Supervisor: Professor Aidan Sims (Honorary)

My bachelor’s degree was awarded at Southern University of Science and Technology, Shenzhen China. My undergraduate academic advisor was Yifei Zhu, whose research interests are algebraic topology and related fields, particularly in its connections to algebraic geometry and number theory via objects such as formal groups, elliptic curves, and modular forms. Currently he is also discussing topics in computational topology and topological data analysis.
My undergraduate thesis supervisor was Bochen Liu , whose research interests are harmonic analysis, geometric measure theory and combinatorics.

item.title: Miscellaneous
item.description:
Interests & Hobbies

Beyond mathematics, here are some things I enjoy:

Music

Music has always been a part of my life. I learned to play the erhu (a traditional Chinese instrument) as a child, and after graduating from high school, I began learning the piano. I love both instruments deeply. My favorite genres include classical music, new age, and traditional Chinese music.

The following is one my piano covers In the Morning Light composed by Yanni. It is the first piece of music totally practiced by myself.

item.title: Work & Thinking
item.description:
Expository Notes

Notes from myself and instructors which I found useful.

The Categorical Origins of Lebesgue Integration

  • Original paper:
    A categorical derivation of Lebesgue integration
    Author: Tom Leinster;

  • Handwritten notes:
    Notes on The Categorical Origins of Lebesgue Integration

Introduction to Representation Theory (Course)

  • Instructor: Jasmin Matz
  • Notes: IntroRep Notes

Groups, Operator Algebras and Dynamics (Project)

  • Instructor: Mikael Rørdam and Magdalena Musat
  • Notes: GOADyn Notes

Graduate Partial Differential Equations (Course)

  • Instructor: Linlin Su
  • Handwritten Notes:
    Graduate PDE Notes 1
    Graduate PDE Notes 2
    Graduate PDE Notes 3

Fourier Analysis, an Introduction

  • Original book: Fourier Analysis, an Introduction
    Author: Elias M. Stein and Rami Shakarchi
  • Handwritten notes:
    Fourier Analysis Notes 1
    Fourier Analysis Notes 2
    Fourier Analysis Notes 3
    Fourier Analysis Notes 4
    Fourier Analysis Notes 5
    Fourier Analysis Notes 6
    Fourier Analysis Notes 7
  • Partial solutions:
    Partial Solutions Part 1
    Partial Solutions Part 2
    Partial Solutions Part 3

Theses

Graduate thesis

C*-algebras of Left Cancellative Small Categories with Garside Families

item.title: Connections between Topological and Categorical Limits
item.description:
Definitions and Examples

Definition (Filter)

A filter on a set $X$ is a collection $\mathcal{F}$ that is

(i) downward directed: $A, B \in \mathcal{F}$ implies there exists $C \in \mathcal{F}$ such that $C \subseteq A \cap B$,
(ii) nonempty: $\mathcal{F} \neq \varnothing$,
(iii) upward closed: $A \in \mathcal{F}$ and $A \subseteq B$ implies $B \in \mathcal{F}$.

An additional property is often useful:
(iv) proper: there exists $A \subseteq X$ such that $A \notin \mathcal{F}$.
